title: The Loafersï؟½ Club
content:
آ آ آ آ A club there is established here, whose name they say is Legion
آ آ آ آ From Melbourne to the Billabong, theyï؟½re known in every region.
آ آ آ آ They do not like the cockatoos, but mostly stick to stations,
آ آ آ آ Where they keep themselves from starving by cadging shepherdsï؟½ rations.

آ آ آ آ The rules and regulations, theyï؟½re not difficult of learning,
آ آ آ آ They are to live upon the cash which others have been earning.
آ آ آ آ To never let a chance go by of being in a shout, sir,
آ آ آ آ And if they see a slant to turn your pockets inside out, sir.

آ آ آ آ Theyï؟½ll cadge your baccy, knife, and pipe, and tell a tale of sorrow
آ آ آ آ Of how they cannot get a job, but mean to start to-morrow.
آ آ آ آ But that to-morrow never comes, until they see quite plainly
آ آ آ آ That itï؟½s completely up the spout with Messrs. Scrase and Ainley.

آ آ آ آ If, feeling thirsty, you should go to take a little suction,
آ آ آ آ Iï؟½ll swear theyï؟½ll not be long before theyï؟½ll force an introduction.
آ آ آ آ One knew you here, one knew you there, all love you like a brother,
آ آ آ آ And if one plan will not succeed, theyï؟½ll quickly try another.

آ آ آ آ I knew one poor, unhappy wight, having a little ready,
آ آ آ آ Entered a Smeaton public-house, determined to keep steady.
آ آ آ آ A celebrated loafer there determined upon showing him
آ آ آ آ That he once had the pleasure and the privilege of knowing him.

آ آ آ آ Through hills and dales, by lakes and streams, he close pursued his victim,
آ آ آ آ Until the miserable man confessed that be quite licked him.
آ آ آ آ In vain the quarry tried to turn, pursuit was far too strong, sir,
آ آ آ آ The loafer followed up the scent and earthed him in Geelong, sir.

آ آ آ آ The noble art of lambing down they know in all its beauty,
آ آ آ آ And if they do not squeeze you dry, theyï؟½ll think theyï؟½ve failed in duty.
آ آ آ آ But, truth to say, they seldom fail to do that duty neatly,
آ آ آ آ And very few escape their hands whoï؟½re not cleared out completely.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Banjo Paterson (Andrew Barton) delivers a powerful performance in "The Loafersï؟½ Club"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
